response.justification: Maria Rodriguez has a broad software engineering background spanning web, mobile, and embedded systems, with genuine strengths in distributed systems and engineering craftsmanship. However, she critically fails to meet the single most important minimum qualification: 8 years of C++ experience. Her C++ exposure appears limited to roughly 6 months at National Instruments and a bachelor's thesis project, with C++ not even listed in her skills section. Additionally, she lacks any cloud platform leadership experience, has no demonstrated experience owning large-scale compute infrastructure, and holds no advanced degree. The role demands a seasoned C++ cloud platform leader, and her profile is primarily a TypeScript/Node.js/DLang full-stack developer.
strengths: Demonstrated distributed systems and high-throughput infrastructure experience at Dunnhumby, handling millions of HTTP requests per second using low-latency techniques
Strong software craftsmanship culture: consistently applied TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and engineering best-practice advocacy across multiple roles
Active technical community contributor through workshops, conference talks, and open-source projects, indicating mentoring aptitude and communication skills
concerns: C++ experience is negligible (~6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 and a thesis project), far short of the 8-year minimum requirement which is the core technical prerequisite
No evidence of cloud compute platform leadership, cloud architecture ownership, or managing platform teams — the central responsibility of this role
No formal technical leadership or direct people-management experience in a cloud or DevOps context; mentoring has been community-based rather than organizational
